Issue Description: Customer unable to log in. Password not accepted after recent system update.
Steps Taken: 1. Verified identity. 2. Confirmed correct email. 3. Sent password reset link. 4. Stayed on line while customer completed reset. 5. Confirmed successful login.
Notes: Customer was relieved and thanked agent. Noted that reset email went to spam.

Issue Description: Customer reports VPN disconnecting every 10-15 minutes since yesterday.
Steps Taken: 1. Confirmed internet connection stable. 2. Had customer update VPN client. 3. Flushed DNS cache. 4. Issue persisted — escalated to Tier 2 with full context.
Notes: Possible ISP-side routing issue. Tier 2 to investigate further.

Issue Description: Customer charged twice for January subscription. Requesting refund.
Steps Taken: 1. Verified identity. 2. Pulled billing history — confirmed duplicate charge. 3. Submitted refund request. 4. Informed customer refund will appear in 3-5 business days.
Notes: Refund reference #RF-2025-0042 issued. Will follow up to confirm receipt.

Issue Description: Customer locked out after 5 failed login attempts.
Steps Taken: 1. Verified identity. 2. Unlocked account in admin portal. 3. Advised customer on password best practices. 4. Confirmed successful login.
Notes: Quick resolution. Customer asked about setting up two-factor authentication — directed to self-service portal.

Issue Description: Application crashes on startup since OS update yesterday.
Steps Taken: 1. Confirmed OS version (Windows 11 update). 2. Had customer clear cache. 3. Uninstall/reinstall app. 4. Issue resolved after reinstall.
Notes: Known issue with recent Win11 patch. Added to knowledge base for future reference.

Issue Description: Customer asking how to export data from the platform.
Steps Taken: 1. Located export feature documentation. 2. Walked customer through steps in real time. 3. Customer confirmed successful export.
Notes: Suggested adding a more visible export button to product feedback log.

Issue Description: Customer wants to update email address on account.
Steps Taken: 1. Verified identity with secondary verification (DOB). 2. Updated email in admin portal. 3. Sent confirmation to new email. 4. Customer confirmed receipt.
Notes: Secondary verification required because primary email no longer accessible.

Issue Description: Customer can connect to VPN but cannot access internal systems.
Steps Taken: 1. Confirmed VPN connection status. 2. Ran DNS flush (ipconfig /flushdns). 3. Confirmed system access restored.
Notes: Classic post-VPN DNS issue. Straightforward resolution.

Steps Taken: 1. Verified identity. 2. Reviewed account — customer eligible for pause option. 3. Presented pause as alternative. 4. Customer chose to pause for 30 days instead of cancelling.
Notes: Customer appreciated the alternative. Scheduled follow-up for Feb 6 to check in.

Issue Description: Customer unable to load any pages within the platform — all other internet works.
Steps Taken: 1. Confirmed internet working. 2. Cleared browser cache/cookies. 3. Tested in different browser — worked. 4. Original browser identified as Chrome with conflicting extension.
Notes: Browser extension (ad blocker) was interfering. Advised customer to whitelist the platform domain.
